message:
🐛 Fix Hardware Health: CORS auth for device inventory + retry recovery (#11043)

- Refactor device handlers (alerts, alerts/clear, inventory) to use
  setCORSHeaders() instead of manual CORS headers. The manual headers
  were missing Access-Control-Allow-Credentials and Access-Control-Max-Age,
  which caused browsers to block credentialed (Authorization header)
  requests even though the corsMiddleware set them — the handler's
  Set() calls overwrote the middleware values without including all
  required CORS headers.

- Add retryFetch() to useCache that resets consecutiveFailures before
  fetching, so the Retry button clears backoff state and immediately
  attempts a fresh fetch. Previously, refetch() kept incrementing
  failures, leaving the UI stuck after MAX_FAILURES.

- Use retryFetch() in HardwareHealthCard's Retry button and
  internationalize the button label via t().

Signed-off-by: ks-ci-bot <ks-ci-bot@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: ks-ci-bot <ks-ci-bot@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

files.patch: @@ -107,7 +107,8 @@ export function HardwareHealthCard() { consecutiveFailures, isDemoFallback, error: fetchError, - refetch } = useCachedHardwareHealth() + refetch, + retryFetch } = useCachedHardwareHealth() const alerts = hwData.alerts const inventory = hwData.inventory @@ -654,12 +655,12 @@ export function HardwareHealthCard() { {fetchError}
